summary: Iter 39 / Phase C — Document upload multi-file + HR Sign-Doc attachments. Full backend + frontend verified end-to-end. 9/9 pytest pass; UI playwright confirms Documents drag-drop queue, 10MB inline error, multi-upload skips oversize while uploading valid ones, modal stays open when any row errors. HR sign-docs: New document editor opens, hr-sd-dropzone + hr-sd-file-input + hr-sd-attached + hr-sd-save all work, card with hr-sign-{id}-attachments + hr-sign-download-{att_id} renders and click triggers download. Backend correctly strips content_base64 from POST response + GET list. Download endpoint returns base64. 10MB cap enforced server-side with 'exceeds 10MB limit'.
verified_flows: POST /api/hr/sign-docs with 2 attachments → 200, attachments list returned WITHOUT content_base64 ✓
GET /api/hr/sign-docs → list projection strips content_base64 from each attachment ✓
GET /api/hr/sign-docs/{id}/attachments/{aid}/download → returns {name, mime_type, content_base64}, decoded payload matches uploaded bytes ✓
Sign-doc no-attachment regression: create → sign → already-signed-400 → delete ✓
/api/documents regression: upload → download (base64 roundtrip) → list contains → delete ✓
UI: /app/documents → upload-doc-btn opens doc-upload-modal with doc-dropzone + doc-file-input ✓
UI: 3-file queue (2 valid + 1 oversize) populates doc-upload-queue; oversize row shows '10.0 MB · max 10 MB' inline ✓
UI: doc-save-btn uploads 2 valid files (emerald check icons), skips oversize (rose error row), modal stays open when any row errors (correct partial-success behavior) ✓
UI: Document cards refresh in list, can be deleted via doc-delete-{id} ✓
UI: /app/hr → hr-tab-sign-docs → hr-sign-new (label 'New document') opens editor ✓
UI: hr-sd-dropzone visible, hr-sd-file-input attaches file, hr-sd-attached shows file row, hr-sd-save → toast 'Document created' ✓
UI: New card carries [data-testid=hr-sign-{id}-attachments] with [data-testid=hr-sign-download-{att_id}], click triggers /api/hr/sign-docs/{id}/attachments/{aid}/download ✓
critical_code_review_comments: Documents.jsx UploadModal.submit(): `const ok = queue.filter((q) => q.status === 'done').length || (valid.length);` reads from the closure-bound `queue` which is stale because setQueue updates trigger re-renders but the local `queue` here references the snapshot at submit() call time. The toast count may be wrong when partial uploads occur. Minor — not blocking. Consider tracking a local counter (`doneCount`) inside the loop and using that for the toast message.

Documents.jsx submit(): the auto-close `setTimeout` checks `q.every((r) => r.status === 'done')` — good. But when even one row is 'error', the modal stays open with no explicit guidance to the user that errored rows are stuck. Minor UX nit.

hr_module.py create_sign_doc: response builder `out = {**doc, 'attachments': [{k: v ...}]}` correctly strips content_base64. Also list_sign_docs uses Mongo projection `attachments.content_base64: 0` — both paths covered ✓.

Code review for hr_module.py — `from typing import ... Any` is missing; line 624 uses `Dict[str, Any]` (in `list_payslips`). Currently works because `Any` is implicitly available via `Dict` in some env, but should be added to the import. (Pre-existing, not from this iter.)
Phase D (Clients module) still pending — out of scope for this iteration but called out by user.
test_credentials: admin@projexino.com / Projexino@2026 — login works, lands /app dashboard. intern creds not exercised in this iter (Phase C is admin-driven).
seed_data_creation: All TEST_QA_* data created during tests was cleaned up (sign-doc deleted, documents deleted via API roundtrip in UI test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 39 Phase C is ship-ready. Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter39_phase_c.py is the regression baseline for HR sign-doc attachments + Documents upload. To run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://projexino-hub.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter39_phase_c.py -v. Next iteration should pick up Phase D Clients module. The known typing.Any import gap in hr_module.py:624 (list_payslips) is pre-existing and not from this iter — flag if you touch that file. Intern role-routing regression from iter38 (intern → /app/dashboard instead of /intern) is still open and unrelated to Phase C.
